---
title: "Adapter Development Guide"
subtitle: "Historical reference ‚Äî pre-DatabaseAdapterBase interface (ComplianceAdapter / NormalizedClaim)"
uiao:
  id: UIAO_ADAPTER_DEV_GUIDE
  status: Needs Replacing
  owner: Governance Board
  tags: [adapter, architecture]
  created: "2026-05-06"
  lastUpdated: "2026-05-06"
summary: >
  Historical reference for the older UIAO adapter interface that predates the
  DatabaseAdapterBase ABC. Uses the ComplianceAdapter base class and NormalizedClaim
  shape. Retained for background context only ‚Äî do not copy the interface for new adapters.
fabric: adapter
---

# Adapter Development Guide

::: {.callout-warning}
## Use the current interface

This guide documents the **pre-`DatabaseAdapterBase`** adapter interface
(`ComplianceAdapter` / `NormalizedClaim`). It is retained for background context
and for understanding older adapters in the codebase.

**For new adapters**, use the current interface:

- [Adapter Authoring Tutorial](adapter-authoring-tutorial.qmd) ‚Äî 30-minute walkthrough
- [Adapter Framework Reference](adapter-framework.qmd) ‚Äî full design rationale and invariants

The current interface (`DatabaseAdapterBase`) supersedes everything documented here.
The seven canonical responsibility domains and the `DatabaseAdapterBase` ABC are
the normative contract. Do not copy the `ComplianceAdapter` shape for new work.
:::

---

## Background ‚Äî why two interfaces exist

The original UIAO adapter interface was designed around a simpler compliance-checking
use case. Adapters subclassed `ComplianceAdapter` and produced `NormalizedClaim` objects ‚Äî
a flatter structure without the provenance chain, schema discovery, or drift detection
that the current system requires.

As UIAO grew from a compliance checker into a full governance platform, the interface
needed to capture:

- **Connection provenance** ‚Äî who connected, when, with what auth method
- **Schema versioning** ‚Äî detecting when the source system's structure changed
- **Drift detection** ‚Äî each adapter reporting its own structural consistency assessment
- **Evidence packaging** ‚Äî pulling evidence by Key Security Indicator

The `DatabaseAdapterBase` ABC (see ADR-015 and ADR-007) introduced these as first-class
responsibility domains. All adapters in the current codebase that were written against
`ComplianceAdapter` have been migrated.

---

## Legacy shape reference

The `ComplianceAdapter` base class defined two methods:

```python
class ComplianceAdapter:
    def collect(self) -> List[NormalizedClaim]:
        """Pull data from the source system and return normalized claims."""
        ...

    def validate(self, claim: NormalizedClaim) -> bool:
        """Return True if the claim passes validation."""
        ...
```

The `NormalizedClaim` dataclass:

```python
@dataclass
class NormalizedClaim:
    control_id: str          # e.g. "AC-1"
    identity: str            # e.g. "policy:DEMO-01"
    status: str              # "pass" | "fail" | "unknown"
    source: str              # adapter ID
    timestamp: datetime
    raw: dict                # raw vendor payload
```

**What it lacked** compared to the current `ClaimObject`:

- No `record_hash` ‚Äî no content integrity check
- No `implementation_status` vocabulary ‚Äî free-form string
- No `evidence_timestamp` ‚Äî separate from collection timestamp
- No `ConnectionProvenance` ‚Äî no audit trail of the connection itself
- No `DriftReport` ‚Äî no per-adapter structural consistency assessment

---

## Migration pattern

If you encounter an older adapter using `ComplianceAdapter`, the migration to
`DatabaseAdapterBase` follows this mapping:

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `ComplianceAdapter.collect()` | Split into `connect()` + `normalize()` |
| `NormalizedClaim.status` | `ClaimObject.implementation_status` (use vocab: `implemented` / `not-implemented` / `unknown`) |
| `NormalizedClaim.raw` | `ClaimObject.raw_payload` |
| *(missing)* | Add `discover_schema()`, `execute_query()`, `detect_drift()`, `collect_evidence()` |
| *(missing)* | Add `record_hash = self._hash(row)` to every claim |

After migration, register the adapter in `adapter-registry.yaml` and add it to
`tests/test_adapter_conformance.py` (the seven-domain conformance suite).

---

## See also

- [Adapter Authoring Tutorial](adapter-authoring-tutorial.qmd) ‚Äî current interface, full walkthrough
- [Adapter Framework](adapter-framework.qmd) ‚Äî design rationale and invariants
- [Appendix A ‚Äî Adapter Plane](appendix/a-adapter-plane/index.qmd) ‚Äî execution environment specification
- ADR-015 ‚Äî Adapter Extensibility (introduces `DatabaseAdapterBase`)
